feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Ariba Supplier Risk
feed.subtitle: SAP Ariba Supplier Risk blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Best Practices for Effective Supplier Risk Management with SAP Ariba Supplier Risk
entry.content:
As supply chains become more complex and intricate,  managing supplier risks is critical for ensuring business continuity, financial stability, regulatory compliance, and overall operational efficiency. Ariba Supplier Risk empowers procurement teams to proactively identify, assess, and mitigate potential risks associated with suppliers. Here are some best practices to leverage Ariba Supplier Risk for optimal results:

1. Data Collection & Integration:

  • Consolidate Data: Strive for a single source of truth by consolidating supplier information from various sources (contracts, internal systems, spend information) into Ariba Supplier Risk using the Risk Category Information API for Supplier Risk Exposure as well as the 300 custom fields.
  • Leverage Third-Party Data: Consider integrating data from third-party data providers to gain insights into a supplier's financial health, sustainability practices, and regulatory compliance. Buy licenses directly with the data provider of your choice and utilize the API mentioned above to integrated data with Ariba Supplier Risk.

Adding third-party data providers

  • Maintain Data Accuracy: Ensure validity and accuracy of the data related to the suppliers through data enrichment process.  

2. Risk Assessment and Prioritization:

  • Define weightage of risk categories & mapping to the input fields: Use the Risk exposure admin page to map input fields (Standard, licensed or custom) based on the type of data available, assign the fields to the risk categories relevant as well as assign weights of the fields towards the risk category calculation as well as towards the overall risk exposure. More details can be found in this page.

  • Identifying critical suppliers to focus risk assessments: Perform abstract risk analysis based on country, commodity, or other criteria to identify the suppliers for whom the risk identification, analysis and mitigation activities need to be performed.

  • Conduct Risk Assessments: Engagement Request process can be used to perform qualitative and quantitative assessment of the suppliers. This Engagement Assessment process can also be used to send questionnaires for assessments related to regulations like LksG (German Supply Chain Law) , EUDR (European Deforestation Laws ) , etc. to all critical suppliers based on the initial risk analysis.

These assessments can be sent to either single supplier or multiple suppliers.

 

  • Prioritized Risk Mitigation activities: Based on the identified risks and criticality of the risks based on the weightage defined, identify the mitigation actions that are required to address the risk.

If collaboration is required for the risk mitigation, Findings and Event Collaboration which is a BTP (Business Technology Platform) service, that is part of SAP Ariba Supplier Risk can be used for creating findings, creating response plan and collaborating with the suppliers for closing the actions as per the response plan. More information related to the provisioning of Findings and Event Collaboration can be found here.

For the identified Risk controls for the Engagement Assessments done, it should be possible to review the effectiveness of the risk controls and the insights will help to determine the next line of action.

Configuring Control effectiveness

3. Regular monitoring and periodic reviews:

Use the periodic review and monitoring option for Engagements to schedule periodic reviews based on the risk levels.

Configuring Periodic Reviews in Engagement Requests

Additional Tips:

  • Establish a clear risk policy statement that defines the process how you will manage your supplier risks, what would be your risk tolerance levels and outline the risk mitigation protocols.
  • Use news alerts and actionable insights to monitor supplier performance and risk indicators to identify any changes or red flags that may require further investigation.

Monitoring of risk exposure

  • Maintain open communication channels with your suppliers to address any concerns and foster a collaborative risk management approach.

By following these best practices and leveraging the comprehensive features of Ariba Supplier Risk, you can effectively manage supplier risk, improve supply chain resilience, and achieve greater operational efficiency within your procurement processes.

References:

Setting up SAP Ariba Supplier Risk:

Setting Up SAP Ariba Supplier Risk | SAP Help Portal

Monitoring Supplier Risk

Monitoring Supplier Risk | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP Procurement (Ariba and Business Network solutions) - Customer Onboarding Webinar Series
entry.content:
The Customer Onboarding Webinar series for Ariba and Business Network for Procurement is split into a series of three webinars:

  • Session one covers how to get started with your new solution
  • Session two covers an overview of your new solution and the services available
  • Session three covers how to engage with Support and prepares you and your team for go-live

We encourage you and your colleagues to attend the series of webinars or watch a past recording via the links below:

Webinar - Live

SAP Intelligent Spend and Business Network

Webinar – On demand

SAP Ariba

Additionally, please visit the SAP Ariba Procurement  for supplementary documentation on your path to success. For SAP Ariba and Business Network for Supply Chain and BN for Commerce Automation , please contact SAP Support Portal Home

SAP Managed Tags:

entry.title: Global Trends in Supply Chain Disruptions
entry.content:
Disruptions in the global supply chain have damaging effects—delayed shipments, empty shelves, businesses scrambling to keep up. It affects everyone, but for businesses, the impact is devastating.

In 2021, at the height of post-COVID supply chain disruptions, Statista reported that US companies lost an average of $228 million on supply chain disruptions. Today things have hardly improved. According to McKinsey, 95% of supply chain professionals reported ongoing challenges in the last year, with geopolitical events, clogged ports, and extreme weather are keeping the pressure on.

But what truly makes businesses vulnerable to supply chain disruptions? It's the economic climate. In 2022, Statista documented the number one reason behind supply chain issues was surging commodity prices. Why? Because high costs and rising interest rates increase the risk of supplier defaults. Even a single supplier failing to deliver can wreak havoc on a supply chain.

In today’s integrated global economy, bringing a product to market involves hundreds, often thousands, of suppliers. If just one of these suppliers faces financial difficulties, the quality, delivery, and profitability of the product can all be put at risk. RapidRatings Data shows a 196% Y-o-Y increase in supplier defaults, which in turn contributes to a sever escalation in supply chain disruption.

So how can companies identify risk within their supply chains? By analyzing the financial health of their suppliers and identifying risk hotspots.

Deep dives into financial health trends across key manufacturing sectors can tell an organization where risk concentrations lie. Take a look at RapidRatings’ data on the automotive industry for example: Transportation Equipment Manufacturing makes up 18% of automotive supply chains, but RapidRatings’ unique FHR data shows that 30% of these suppliers are high risk. Armed with this knowledge, automotive companies can prioritize due diligence and deeper financial analysis of these critical suppliers, ensuring a more resilient supply chain.

RapidRatings core business is a predictive analytics model that examines the full financial statement of public and private companies to identify underlying financial risk. Why is this significant? Financial health is relevant because it underpins all other risk domains.

When a company has poor or deteriorating financial health it’s forced to compromise important factors such as quality, standards, regulatory and delivery logistics. This exposes your business to exceptional production, delivery and reputational risks. Very quickly, their problems become your problems - unless you have insights that give you time to mitigate effectively.

Organizations are increasingly recognizing the importance of financial health monitoring that goes beneath the superficial and examines the suppliers actual financial statements. It’s now a key building block for a more stable and resilient supply chain. According to Statista, 15% of organizations with significant supply chain operations plan to implement operational technology for this very purpose – the highest adoption rate of any category.

By proactively managing supplier risks, businesses can keep supply chain disruptions at bay and safeguard their bottom line.

Learn more about RapidRatings and SAP integrated financial health solution by visiting SAP App Store.

entry.title: Embracing Sustainability with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Introduction

Sustainability has been defined as “meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs” by UN World Commission on Environment and Development. In practical terms, it can mean the ability to manage natural resources well over the long term. Better processes & systems can help in managing today's needs without depleting resources or causing harm to the environment, economy, or society. Sustainability involves 3 main aspects of Environment, Socials & Economics.

  • Environmental Sustainability aims at conserving natural resources, reducing pollution, and protecting ecosystems.
  • Social Sustainability focuses on human rights, fair labor practices & seeks to ensure that everyone has equal access to resources and opportunities necessary for a healthy and fulfilling life.
  • Economic Sustainability is about creating systems and practices to balance economic development & resource use while maintaining a healthy environment.

The essence of Sustainability is to create a harmonious balance between these 3 pillars to ensure that current and future generations can thrive without compromising the planet's health or societal well-being.

From an organizational perspective, there is increasing consumer pressure on companies to target Carbon Zero Future. There are more & more regulations (like European Sustainability Reporting Standards or ESRS, Human Rights Due Diligence, Supply Chain Act Germany) to meet targets in line with Paris Agreement & COP26. Investor expectations are rising for significant Scope 3 (indirect emissions) reduction efforts. Up to 90% of a company’s environmental footprint lies in its supply chain – meaning that the supply chain represents a major opportunity for companies to further their environmental, social and governance (ESG) objectives. Refer Value chain and supply chain sustainability for details.

The purpose of this post is to highlight the topic of Sustainability for discussions. It provides brief introduction to the subject, touches upon the Sustainability solutions from SAP and provides high level inputs & resources around Sustainable Procurement with SAP Ariba.

 

SAP & Sustainability

SAP's purpose is to help the world run better and improve people's lives with sustainability at the core. In line with it, SAP offers a wide array of Sustainability solutions for its customers to embrace sustainability effectively. Solutions span across topics like ESG reporting, Climate action, Circular economy, Social responsibility, CSRD & Sustainable procurement. With these solutions, there is an opportunity to positively influence 12 of the 17 UN defined sustainability goals. Some of the prominent sustainability solutions from SAP are as below. These are SAP Sustainability solutions focused around SAP S/4HANA / SAP S/4HANA Cloud and we talk about SAP Ariba related sustainability solutions in the next section.

Green Ledger

It is a solution designed to help businesses accurately account for the carbon they produce across their value chain. It integrates environmental data into financial processes, thus enabling organizations to manage their sustainability performance as effectively as they manage the financials. For quick product overview, refer this 3 minutes video.

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM)

This solution helps businesses measure, manage, and reduce their environmental impact along the value chain. It enables organizations to calculate and track their carbon footprint, water usage, and other environmental metrics. It is a cloud application running on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). For quick product overview, refer this 2 minutes video.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT)

It is cloud solution to help businesses monitor and manage their sustainability performance. It helps organizations to track, analyze, and optimize their sustainability initiatives & achieve goals like reducing carbon footprint, delivering on green line performance, and meeting diversity and inclusion targets. For quick product overview, refer this 2 minutes video.

SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (SDX)

It is a solution designed to enable secure and efficient exchange of sustainability-related data between businesses and their partners. It aims to enhance collaboration, compliance & transparency across the entire supply chain by enabling organizations to share and access verified sustainability data seamlessly. It is a based on SAP Business Network & built using the technical framework of GreenToken by SAP.

It standardizes data exchange by following technical specifications of the Partnership for Carbon Transparency (PACT) framework of the World Business Council of Sustainable Development (WBSCD). It also offers interoperability with other equivalent networks using WBCSD PACT standards. Companies can use SAP SDX along with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Alternatively, if businesses already have a solution that calculates carbon footprints effectively, they can use SAP SDX to share this data securely. For more details on the solution, kindly refer these links.

  • Quick Product Overview (2 minutes video)
  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange

 

SAP Ariba & Sustainability

Adopting digitalization helps in sustainability by providing the tools and insights needed to operate more efficiently, reduce waste, and make informed decisions that benefit the environment and society.  For overall guidance on embracing Sustainability in procurement processes, refer this 10 minutes video.

Using SAP Ariba in itself reduces paper work and help with resource optimization contributing to sustainability. Also, with its wide range of solutions & strong collaboration capabilities, SAP Ariba can help an organization further to achieve its Sustainability goals while working with the supplier base. Additionally, new solutions & features help to take it even further. Below figure attempts to present various ideas through which SAP Ariba can contribute to Sustainability goals.

Embedding Sustainability in Source to Pay Process

 

Other Related Topics

Sustainable Supplier Finance

Sustainable Supplier Finance program helps & finances buyers to incentivize supplier who adopt sustainable practices. Buyers can apply a pricing benefit for suppliers who meet predefined sustainability criteria. Taulia, a SAP company, offers solution to make this effective for companies. Buyer can easily track the suppliers’ ESG performance and incentivize them to improve it. Kindly refer this link for more details.

Solution Extension - Sustainability for SAP Ariba

This solution offers SAP Ariba customers the ability to enhance functionality and integrate applications that support procurement sustainability goals. It is a Partner solution from Cordis Solutions, a partner company of excelerateds2p & is available on SAP Store. Solution is a comprehensive collection of packaged services, pre-built content, enhanced configuration, and complimentary extensions built on BTP to support a new or existing Ariba customer on their sustainability journey. For more details, refer Sustainability for SAP Ariba

Ecovadis

EcoVadis is the universally recognized assessment platform which rates businesses' sustainability based on 4 key categories: environmental impact, labor & human rights standards, ethics, and procurement practices. It provides 2 solution extensions as below for SAP Ariba which are available on SAP Store.

  • EcoVadis Business Sustainability Assessments, ESG Ratings and Carbon Scorecards

This SAP Store App for Suppliers can help them to add their sustainability rating in SAP Business Network profile. It can help Suppliers stay compliant & also boost their sales. You can also refer to this Blog Post from Amanda @amandalaldin for more details - Standing out as a sustainable supplier with SAP Business Network and EcoVadis

SAP Business Network Supplier Profile with Ecovadis Ratings

  •  EcoVadis Business Sustainability Ratings for SAP Ariba

This SAP Store App integrated with SAP Ariba Supplier Risk helps you review the ESG risk and performance of your Suppliers with robust and reliable sustainability scorecards, and take action to reduce the carbon footprint. Risk scorecard can directly be seen from the Ariba SLP Supplier 360 dashboard. This direct integration helps to make informed decisions & take positive actions based on sustainability targets resulting in higher efficiency and more sustainable value chains.

Risk Scores in SAP Ariba SLP Supplier 360

AI & Sustainability

Sustainability & Profitability go hand in hand, and AI can play a crucial role in embedding them together. SAP aims at using SAP Business AI to help customers optimize for sustainable impact across their full value chain, enabling the intelligent, sustainable enterprise, powered by the pool of foundational business data. AI can help deciding on data-driven ESG and net-zero goals, strategies, and actions. It can also help complying with regulations and automate sustainability reporting. Few use cases already exist and others can be built using SAP BTP. Kindly refer below resources for more details.

  • The power of sustainability, ERP, and AI - This 6 minute video explains how integrating sustainability into ERP business processes, combined with the power of SAP Business AI, can help an organization’s business decision-making to the next level and meet environmental, social, and governance compliance requirements.
  • Building AI and Sustainability Solutions on SAP BTP - This SAP Learning (learning.sap.com) Course helps building skills to infuse AI in Sustainability solutions. Its around 10 Hours in duration and is free for all.
  • Putting Sustainability at the Heart of ERP - SAP recently announced 2 new AI-driven sustainability use cases - Emission Factor Mapping with AI & ESG Report Generation with AI. This article has more details.

 

Conclusion

In today's world, sustainability is no longer an option but a necessity for organizations aiming to thrive and remain competitive. SAP Ariba provides a platform to help businesses achieve business goals while integrating sustainability into their procurement and supply chain operations. It helps businesses to make informed decisions that positively impact the environment while driving economic value.

By adopting SAP Ariba, digital transformation and sustainability can go hand-in-hand. Organizations can contribute to a more sustainable world and create long-term value for all stakeholders. In summary, embracing sustainability with SAP Ariba is not just about compliance and efficiency, it's about redefining business success in the context of our global responsibility. By embedding sustainable practices into core operations, businesses can pave the way for a more sustainable and prosperous future for all. It can help the world run better and improve people's lives with sustainability at the core.

 

Consolidated List of Resources

  • SAP's Sustainability solutions
  • UN defined sustainability goals
  • Green Ledger Overview video
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM) Overview video
  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) Overview video
  • SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (SDX) Overview video
  • Sustainability in Procurement Processes video
  • Sustainable Supplier Finance
  • Solution Extension - Sustainability for SAP Ariba
  • EcoVadis Business Sustainability Assessments, ESG Ratings and Carbon Scorecards
  • EcoVadis Business Sustainability Ratings for SAP Ariba
  • The power of sustainability, ERP, and AI video
  • Training Course - Building AI and Sustainability Solutions on SAP BTP
  • Article - Putting Sustainability at the Heart of ERP

 

Note: Kindly refer 'Comments' for updates to the Blog Post. Also requesting readers to enter Comments with info which can be helpful to all.

entry.title: Enhanced Continuous Quality Check for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Now Available
entry.content:
“Continuous quality checks” are individual services delivered by SAP Enterprise Support and are available with every cloud subscription. The remote-service reports help you reduce technical risks and identify areas for optimization potential, based on real-life data from your systems or solution.

The revamped Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for deployment readiness for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network is now available. This CQC is designed to provide an SAP best practice review for key settings in your SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network instance.

View the CQC info sheet for full details.

Solutions Covered by This Service

  • SAP Ariba Sourcing
  • SAP Ariba Contracts
  • SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance
  • SAP Ariba Supplier Risk NEW
  • SAP Ariba Buying
  • SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  • SAP Ariba Catalog
  • SAP Ariba Invoice Management
  • SAP Business Network for Procurement
  • SAP Business Network Commerce Automation NEW
  • SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration NEW
  • SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network NEW

Key Benefits

  • Review current settings, use of features, and enablement of key functions.
  • Identify concerns, issues and potential risks prior to deployment/go-live.
  • Receive a detailed service report that highlights key areas for consideration and SAP best practice recommendations.
  • Optimize the possibility for a smooth start of production.

When to Use

  • Before the go-live of a new SAP Ariba implementation.
  • When additional solutions are added to the existing SAP Ariba suite.
  • As a "health check" for productive SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network systems.

How to Request

  • Remote Services can be requested via the new Get Support application in SAP for Me (SAP Note 1296527). When submitting your request, please select:
  1. Product: “Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services”
  2. Product function: “Service request for a Cloud Solution”
  • If you need assistance in submitting your case, you may contact your local Customer Interaction Center (CIC).
  • SAP recommends service booking of six (6) weeks prior to go-live date, to allow for the delivery four (4) weeks before your go-live date and provide adequate time to review and implement any SAP recommendations (attempts will be made to accommodate requests made outside this timeframe, but are not guaranteed).
  • SAP will evaluate if this service is suitable for your business needs.
  • SAP recommends the service to be performed on the productive tenant, if not available it can be performed on the test tenant.

Achieve More with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Find the right content to accelerate the value of your solution with the Spend Management and Business Network value map from SAP Enterprise Support. Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can navigate to curated content, be directed to self-service platforms, engage with fellow customers and SAP experts, and discover continuous quality checks to mitigate implementation risks or identify optimization potential.

Explore how SAP technology can transform your procurement operation or make your existing software work harder for you by visiting value maps today.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:   

  1. One-time sign-up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required   
  2. Access value maps 
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value map for Spend Management and Business Network 

Once in the value map, we welcome you to collaborate in our forums, ask questions or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further help. Value maps are easy to navigate but an on-boarding call allows us to tailor the conversation to suit your needs and highlight specific content for you. 

Got a question about value maps, or our content?

📺Watch our short video.

Leverage the “ask questions” feature in the value map or email us:
sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

entry.title: SAP Ariba Supplier Management 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community! We’re excited to introduce some fantastic new features in Supplier Management and Risk, crafted to enhance your experience and improve efficiency. Let’s dive right into these updates and see how they can make a difference in your procurement processes.

Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis (ARI-20913)

Ever wanted a broader insight into your suppliers’ risks based on their geographical and commodity attributes? The Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis feature allows SAP Ariba Supplier Risk users to precisely do that. You can now view suppliers segmented by country/region and commodity risk and even export these segmentation reports to a spreadsheet. This granular visibility aids in making well-informed decisions, ensuring your supply chain is robust against potential vulnerabilities and allowing for the prioritization of suppliers requiring additional analysis and action.

The Segmented View page with the new Create Engagement Request buttonSelf-Assessed Questionnaire Version Upgrade Notification (ARI-22401)

 

Keeping your assessments up-to-date is crucial. With the Self-Assessed Questionnaire Version Upgrade Notification, when a buyer sends a modular questionnaire for supplier assessment, the system compares the existing version in SAP Ariba Supplier Risk with the version on the SAP Business Network. If the latter is more recent, an alert icon is displayed, and the authorized buyer administrator receives an email prompt. This feature ensures you always utilize the most current assessment version, maintaining alignment and accuracy in your assessment process.

Multiple Supplier Engagement Request Status and Administrative Functionalities (ARI-22417)

Efficiency is key, especially when managing multiple engagements. The Multiple Supplier Engagement Request Status and Administrative Functionalities feature allows you to monitor the status of Bulk Engagement Requests and Send Assessments. Additionally, it includes retry options for any failed requests, ensuring smooth and continuous operations without missing a beat.

Risk Exposure Calculation Model Improvements (ARI-22870)

Risk assessments just got more comprehensive. The updated Risk Exposure Calculation Model now combines the incident and supplier models to provide a robust view of a supplier’s potential risk exposure. By integrating data from both models, this enhancement presents a clearer and more precise risk landscape, empowering you to take more proactive measures.

The new Incident Mapping tab

Filter Alerts by Risk Category (ARI-23782)

Navigating through numerous alerts can be daunting. The new Filter Alerts by Risk Category feature introduces a search filter for the Risk Category in the Alerts area. This enables more refined searches based on one or more risk categories, helping you zero in on the most critical alerts and respond swiftly.

Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure (ARI-23844)

Customization is at the heart of personalized risk management. The Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure feature allows you to import and utilize your own country risk score information, rather than relying solely on the default World Economic Forum (WEF) data. This flexibility ensures you can configure risk exposure of suppliers using the most relevant and specific data to your organization.

Activity Status in the Supplier Profile Summary (NGSM-3108)

Quick access to a supplier's activity status is now a reality. The Activity Status in the Supplier Profile Summary feature prominently displays if a supplier is inactive or has a common block directly in the header of their profile summary. This visibility ensures you are always aware of the supplier’s current status, supporting smooth transaction and supplier management activities.

Navigation Between Guided Sourcing Pages and the Supplier Profile Summary (NGSM-4725)

Experience seamless navigation between sourcing workflows with the Navigation Between Guided Sourcing Pages and the Supplier Profile Summary. Category managers and sourcing users can now open the profile summary from common guided sourcing workflows and utilize breadcrumbs for easy navigation back to events and other pages. This integration streamlines your workflow, making information access and task execution more efficient.

We believe these features will significantly enhance your supplier management and risk assessment processes, bringing more control, efficiency, and customization to your fingertips. Dive in and explore these new functionalities today! Stay connected with us by subscribing to our content and using the links below for more updates and feel free to reach out to your SAP Ariba support team for any assistance.

  • Register for the Early Release Series for 2408 release
  • Resources:
    • Feature related materials
      • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
      • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
    • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
    • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
    • What’s New
  • Encourage customers to check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates.
  • Introduce customers to
    • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
    • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2408 Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We’re thrilled to introduce several innovative features designed to enhance your procurement processes. These updates focus on improving visibility, efficiency, and integration across our SAP Ariba solutions. For those of you looking for a deeper dive into specific functionalities, we’ll also provide links to detailed blogs that explore a more comprehensive view for specific areas. Let's dive right in!

Master Data Overview in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (CIM-1428)

With the new Display Imported Master Data app, you can now view all master data along with replication details imported to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management from connected systems. This feature addresses the need for visibility over replicated master data, ensuring that you can validate and monitor data replication efficiently.

Extract Multiple PO References at Header Level (CIM-431)

Invoice processing just got a lot smoother. The Extract at Header Level Multiple PO References from Invoice feature utilizes SAP AI Business Services' Document Information Extraction to pull multiple PO reference values directly at the header level of an invoice. This advancement enhances automation and accuracy in handling invoices, helping you save time and reduce manual workload.

Duplicate Invoice Detection (CIM-220)

Enhance the reliability of your invoice processing automation with the Duplicate Invoice Detection feature. This function checks fields such as company code, invoicing party, external reference, and invoice date in draft invoices against existing invoices during editing or transfer. It automatically flags duplicates, preventing errors and potential fraud.

Purchase Requisition Approvals in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App (SAS-318)

Efficiency on the go! With the Support for Purchase Requisition Approvals in the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app, approvers can easily search for requisitions in their to-do list, view line-item details, and approve or reject them through a role-based user interface. This feature ensures that crucial approval workflows are accessible anytime, anywhere. Read more about how this feature can help streamline your mobile procurement processes in SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2408 Release Key Innovations

SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App The - Purchase Requisition Approvals screen

 

Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis (ARI-20913)

Gain critical insights into your suppliers' risks with the Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis feature. This tool allows you to view and analyze suppliers based on country/region and commodity risk, and easily export segmentation reports to a spreadsheet.

The Segmented View page with the new Create Engagement Request button

 

Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure (ARI-23844)

Customize your risk assessment with the Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure feature. You now have the flexibility to use your own source of country risk score information, rather than solely relying on default data. Tailor your risk exposure configurations to better align with your organization's specific criteria and needs. For more information on SAP Ariba Supplier Management features, take a look at the SAP Ariba Supplier Management 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

Viewing Supplier Risk Level in SAP Ariba Category Management (CAT-5800)

Understanding supplier risk levels is vital for strategic category management. The new integration between SAP Ariba Category Management and SAP Ariba Supplier Risk allows category managers to view and assess supplier risk levels (Low, Medium, High). This visibility is essential for making informed decisions and strategizing effectively.

The new Risk Level available in the Category Suppliers page

Integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers (CAT-7893)

Enhance your cost structure analysis with the ability to integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers. Category management administrators can now incorporate external cost structure data directly into the Cost Structure tool within the strategy and plan document. This addition allows for more comprehensive and accurate strategy formulation. To delve deeper into this and other SAP Ariba Category Management, SAP Ariba guided sourcing, and SAP Ariba Contract Management, check out our SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

With these features, we are aiming to streamline your procurement processes, enhance risk management, and provide deeper insights into supplier relationships. We encourage you to explore these exciting updates and implement them into your workflows to harness their full potential. Stay tuned for more updates by subscribing to myself and our other fantastic bloggers and take a look at the links below.

  • Register for the Early Release Series for 2408 release
  • Resources:
    • Feature related materials
      • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
      • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
    • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
    • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
    • What’s New
  • Encourage customers to check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates.
  • Introduce customers to
    • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
    • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: 2408 Edition of the Release Navigator for Spend Management and Business Network
entry.content:
The Readiness@Scale team is pleased to announce that the 2408 release content and UX updates will be are available in the Release Navigator for Spend Management and Business Network now. This update will showcase the latest content to help you prepare for new innovations, as well as a UX update that enhances site navigation. Additionally, a new Release Checklist will be included to ensure that you are fully prepared to implement new innovations.

2408 Feature Highlights

Here are key feature highlights for the 2405 Release, make sure to check out other key features in the SAP Ariba Key Innovation Highlights Blog:

  • IM-1428 Master Data Overview in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management 
  • CIM-431  Extract Multiple PO References at Header Level 
  • CIM-220 Duplicate Invoice Detection 
  • SAS-318  Purchase Requisition Approvals in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App
  • ARI-20913 Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis 
  • ARI-23844 Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure 
  • CAT-5800 Viewing Supplier Risk Level in SAP Ariba Category Management 
  • CAT-7893 Integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers

2408 UX Updates

The 2408 Release will incorporate UX updates based on the feedback received throughout the year. On July 29th, the release navigator will now have a clean & simple homepage with side menu options for easier navigation. The new side menu has 4 different pages to choose from:

  • Product Specific Content: This is the new landing page of the Release Navigator where users can find updated messages and information in the welcome box, upcoming release dates from the release calendar, and current release related resources broken down by product.
  • General Users can find general resources that assist with their SAP Cloud Solution like SAP for Me, SAP Activate, SAP Value Maps, and more. This is also where users can find the other Release Navigators.
  • Get Assistance: You can find navigation tips, the community blog post for the Release Navigator, translation feature, and user feedback.
  • User Feedback: This is where users can provide feedback on the content being provided, suggestions on the Release Navigator, or any other feedback that one has.

Access Points

Support.SAP.com – Release Ready page 

SAP Activate – Roadmap Viewer  

SAP Enterprise Support Value Map - SAP Enterprise Support Value Map for Spend Management and Business Network

Making Your Impact

At SAP, we greatly appreciate your input and suggestions. You are invited to submit feedback and suggest topics to be covered through our Topic Request & Feedback Survey. This survey provides you with an opportunity to provide insights on your experience with the release navigator for Spend Management and Business Network, as well as suggestions on how we can further enhance your release experience. Additionally, we encourage you to share your feedback, thoughts, or questions in the comment section below. 

Staying Connected

Follow the Spend Management and Business Network and ReadinessS@Scale community tags, and Ryan Russman to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: Understanding The Cost of Supply Chain Disruption
entry.content:
Supply chains are the backbone of many industries and businesses. Every part, from raw materials to operational technology, must arrive on time and meet the exact specifications to keep production running smoothly. When supply chain disruption strikes, the impacts are devastating. In 2021, during the height of post-COVID supply chain disruptions, the average cost of supply chain disruptions for an organization was $228 million according to Statista. 

Factors such as persistent inflation and high interest rates mean that supply chain risks are still alive and well today. According to Deloitte, the leading cause of supply chain issues in Central Europe was higher prices for commodities. This is because the majority of suppliers are private companies with finite resources, so, when inflationary pressures hit, their cash reserves are squeezed and they become less resilient against unforeseen disruption. 

In today’s economic landscape, how can companies gauge risk levels across their entire supply chain? Through financial health monitoring.  

By analyzing the financial statements of their suppliers, organizations understand whether their suppliers have the resources to withstand unexpected challenges. And they can take proactive steps to mitigate risk. At a broader level, companies can observe risk in portfolios or categories of suppliers and detect risk concentrations effectively.   

RapidRatings analysis of supplier financials across four key sectors in manufacturing shows where risk is heavily concentrated: 

  • Automotive: The bulk of the automotive supply chain is Transportation Equipment Manufacturing, making up about 18% of the supply chain. However,  RapidRatings data shows that 30% of suppliers in this sector are high risk or very high risk. 
  • Healthcare and Pharma: One of the most critical supplier sectors to the pharma and healthcare industries is Scientific Research and Development Services, as it drives and innovation and development in drugs and devices. Concerningly, 24% of these suppliers are at high risk or very high risk based on their ability to withstand financial pressure and other challenges.   
  • Aerospace and Defense: Aerospace Product and Parts Manufacturing is a vital segment in the aerospace and defense supply chain, yet 36% of suppliers have a high or very high risk of defaulting – representing a substantial level of exposure to disruption.  
  • Technology: Close to 20% of the semiconductor and other electronic component manufacturing sector are high or very high risk based on analysis of their financial health. 

By implementing comprehensive financial health monitoring, organizations can identify high risk suppliers and manage their exposure to disruption risk. RapidRatings’ FHR is a financial health assessment that provides ratings on private and public suppliers using financial statements, giving companies a comprehensive view of risk levels across their entire supply chain  

Understanding supplier’s financial health is not solely about avoiding or reducing exposure to risk, but about building strategic partnerships. Financial assessments allow you to partner with strong suppliers, increase longer-term commitments, and create mutual growth opportunities.  

In today's volatile economic climate, supply chain disruption is a constant threat. By prioritizing financial health monitoring and building robust supplier relationships, businesses can significantly reduce their exposure to risk and gain a competitive advantage.

entry.title: Future proofing your business with procurement technology
entry.content:
Watch our intro video before you start 

What’s Your Procurement Strategy Persona?

Please record your answers offline to define your persona

1. When it comes to choosing suppliers, your priority is:

  • A) Price and cost savings.
  • B) Quality and reliability.
  • C) Innovation and technology.
  • D) Long-term relationships and collaboration.

2. How do you typically handle contract compliance?

  • A) We focus on price terms and conditions.
  • B) We have a robust monitoring system in place.
  • C) We leverage technology for real-time tracking.
  • D) We emphasize mutual trust and flexibility.

3. What is your approach to managing procurement categories?

  • A) Grouping similar products for bulk purchasing.
  • B) Detailed analysis and specialized management for each category.
  • C) Using advanced software for dynamic category management.
  • D) Collaborative planning with suppliers.

4. In terms of supplier risk management, you:

  • A) Regularly review financial stability and performance.
  • B) Focus on diversifying your supplier base.
  • C) Use predictive analytics to foresee potential risks.
  • D) Build strong, long-term relationships to mitigate risks.

5. What’s your stance on payment terms with suppliers?

  • A) Negotiating the best possible payment terms to improve cash flow.
  • B) Ensuring fair and timely payments to maintain good relationships.
  • C) Utilizing technology to manage and optimize payment schedules.
  • D) Customizing payment terms based on individual supplier agreements.

6. How do you see the role of AI in procurement?

  • A) AI can help reduce costs and find the best deals.
  • B) AI can improve the accuracy and efficiency of procurement processes.
  • C) AI is crucial for gaining strategic insights and recommendations.
  • D) AI can enhance collaboration and decision-making with suppliers.

7. When planning for the future, your main concern is:

  • A) Keeping costs low and maintaining budget control.
  • B) Ensuring the highest quality of goods and services.
  • C) Staying ahead of the competition with cutting-edge technology.
  • D) Building a resilient and adaptable supply chain.

8. What’s your approach to dealing with supply chain disruptions?

  • A) Having a backup plan and alternative suppliers ready.
  • B) Focusing on supplier reliability and performance.
  • C) Utilizing advanced analytics to predict and respond to disruptions.
  • D) Collaborating closely with suppliers to develop contingency plans.

9. How important is it for you to integrate technology in your procurement process?

  • A) It’s important for cost tracking and savings.
  • B) It’s essential for maintaining high standards and efficiency.
  • C) It’s critical for staying innovative and competitive.
  • D) It’s valuable for improving supplier relationships and collaboration.

10. What’s your biggest procurement challenge right now?

  • A) Managing and reducing costs.
  • B) Ensuring contract compliance and quality.
  • C) Keeping up with technological advancements.
  • D) Building and maintaining strong supplier relationships.

Please review your corresponding personality type:

  • Mostly As: Cost Controller 
  • Mostly Bs: Quality Guardian 
  • Mostly Cs: Tech Innovator 
  • Mostly Ds: Relationship Builder 

If you have the same number of answers in two or more personality types, please review all of the videos related to your personalities.

Now you're a step closer towards transforming your procurement organization.

entry.title: End-to-End Due Diligence in Supply Chain
entry.content:
End-to-end supplier due diligence is the foundation of resilient supply chains. The process (from selection to segmentation and management) involves carefully evaluating and selecting suppliers based on their ability to fulfill your business needs.  

The world’s largest companies, from Apple to Nvidia, perform due diligence to ensure they align with the strongest suppliers. But with many supplier risk areas to assess—from quality and assurance to cyber readiness and ESG adherence—how can organizations streamline the process and detect when risk accurately? By focusing on supplier financial health.  

Financial health assessments underpin all risk areas. A supplier facing financial challenges compromises quality, cybersecurity, and regulatory compliance. These issues trickle through the supply chain, impact your operations and damage your credibility. 

Organizations can protect themselves by incorporating financial assessments, such as financial health ratings. Financial health ratings, or FHR’s, analyze the financial statements of a supplier and assess their operational resilience and risk of default and disruption. They give businesses the insights and expertise to determine whether a supplier is a strong partner in the long-term or is likely to let them down. 

Baking financial health assessment into due diligence process has multiple benefits: 

Stronger risk mitigation: A supplier's financial health tells you more about their core strength and operational ability to respond to the many external and internal pressures businesses come under. 

Regulatory compliancy: All supply chains are subject to numerous regulations including labor laws, esg, quality and safety standards. Suppliers with insufficient means struggle to uphold regulatory requirements exposing your business to risk of penalties. 

  • Transparency: Visibility into a supplier’s financial statements fosters greater levels of trust and strengthens the relationship. 
  • Consistency: Sustained quality across batches of materials and components if reliant on the financial health of the supplier and their ability to purchase adequate raw materials.  
  • Cost efficiency: These measures combine to reduce the risk of defects, recalls, late deliveries, production disruption and the need to back-fill orders or replace a supplier at short notice. These failures are avoidable costs.  

While initial due diligence is essential, it is equally important to maintain ongoing financial oversight of your suppliers. A supplier that was financially sound during the onboarding process might encounter challenges later, especially given today’s economic factors of persistent inflation and high interest rates. Implementing regular financial health assessments (recommended twice-yearly reviews) helps ensure that your partners remain stable and reliable. 

By consistently monitoring your suppliers' financial performance, you can ensure that risk is mitigated, operations are streamlined, and costs stay down by keeping disruption at bay. 

In today’s volatile market, end-to-end due diligence is no longer a nice-to-have, it’s a necessity. By implementing financial health assessments into due diligence processes, you can protect your business from a wide range of supplier risks and strengthen your operation.

entry.title: Real-Time Support for Procurement Webcast: Introduction to the SAP Community!
entry.content:
Real-Time Support for Procurement Webcast Series

SAP Support's Digital Strategy team successfully hosted a live webcast on Aug 22, 2024. During this live session, we covered:

  • Why and how you should use the SAP Community
  • How to get registered
  • Asking questions to find help
  • Finding and following relevant tags 
  • Commenting & Answering 
  • Blogs on the SAP Community
  • Gamification, direct messages, and settings 
  • Where to find resources to become an SAP Community super-user! 

Watch or rewatch the webcast here (17:45, English)!

Download the slides

Get registered today!

 

What is the SAP Community and why should you register?

SAP Community Tour

Benefits to joining with absolutely no cost:

  • Connect and engage with like-minded professionals
  • Ask questions and share your product knowledge
  • Participate in discussions with SAP experts
  • Follow topics that align with your interests and/or location
  • Learn and stay current on SAP products through blogs
  • Explore and join groups, events, and fun contests

As a user of SAP Ariba, SAP Business Network, and/or SAP Fieldglass products, check out the Spend Management content page. Here, you can Search, look through the different boards for Q&A or Blogs, see recent feed, find learning material, and more. 

 

Why use the SAP Community for Procurement products?

There are a lot of different Support options and tools available to you, no matter how you use SAP products. Find how-to information, FAQs, Documentation, tutorials, and guided help in our Help Centers for Ariba Suppliers/ Buyers or Fieldglass users. 

Getting Support from SAP is easy and useful, but there are times when you want advice or guidance from fellow users. Maybe your site configuration is unique and you want to see how other companies handle a certain site setting, or you are looking for best practices about enabling a new feature, or you're an integrated customer looking for help on multiple SAP solutions. 

The SAP Community is completely free to use and offers you a vast network of users and experts! 

 

Your turn!

Subscribe to tags to customize your experience. 

Click on one of the tags from this blog directly to start following content, or Subscribe manually: 

  1. Visit the SAP Community.
  2. Click Products and Technology > All SAP Managed Tags.
  3. Find and click on a tag you're interested in.
  4. Click Subscribe. 

Look for all our tags starting with SAP Ariba, SAP Business Network, and SAP Fieldglass! 

Ask questions for help, get advice on best practices, and consult with other SAP users and experts. 

A well-written question includes:

  • Clear & descriptive title
  • Steps taken that led to your question
  • Screenshots & specific error messages 
  • Specific details (product version, customizations, roles or user authorizations, etc)
  • Correct SAP managed tag(s) added
  • No personally identifiable information (vendor details, user contact information, account numbers, etc)

Share your product knowledge and showcase your expertise by answering questions and commenting. 

Follow the SAP Community Rules of Engagement whenever you are participating.

See all our how-to videos

Hope to see you engaging in the Spend Management area of SAP Community soon! 

Resources from the webcast: 

  • Access the on-demand presentation
  • SAP Community tour
  • How-to Community video tutorials
  • Rules of Engagement

 

Real-Time Support for Procurement Webcast Series

Check out our webcast calendar for upcoming presentations!
Did you miss a webcast? Review our archive for all past content!

entry.title: 2411 Early Release Series for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network releases
entry.content:
Sign up now for the 2411 Early Release Series

SAP strives to equip our customers and partners with valuable release readiness resources for each SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network quarterly release.

Follow this blog to receive notification of new series as soon as registration opens.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Early Release Series
The SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Early Release series of webcasts cover features planned for the upcoming quarterly release.  This platform, available to customers and partners, has a clear mission: deliver an interactive forum to engage with SAP product experts providing simple, direct answers to your most pressing questions.  There is a different session for each product area with content tailored for the specific audience:

  • Supplier Management & Risk
  • Procure-to-Pay
  • Source-to-Contract
  • Business Network

If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts, we encourage you to participate in these informative sessions.  Register now!

Quarterly Release Communications
Notice of upcoming quarterly releases will be communicated to Buyer customers three weeks prior to the release date.  Suppliers receive notice two weeks prior.  Releases are communicated via email notification (look for subject line starting with: Advance Release Notice).

Designated Support Contacts (DSC) and anyone from your organization that has chosen to subscribe to product notifications will receive these announcements. 

The release calendar for SAP Business Network  and release calendar SAP Ariba Applications identify release timing several quarters in advance.

Where to find out more details?
On demand replays of webcasts from completed quarterly releases can be consumed from Release Readiness Resources: Webcast Archive Page

entry.title: SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance & Supplier Risk 2411 Release: Key Feature Highlights
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We're excited to unveil the innovative features included in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance as well as Supplier Risk for the upcoming 2411 release. These enhancements are designed to further streamline your supplier management processes and risk assessment capabilities. Let's dive into the details of these new functionalities!


Intelligent Recommendations Based on Supplier Risk Score

SAP Ariba Supplier Risk now provides intelligent recommendations for suppliers experiencing significant changes in their risk scores. This feature ensures that buyers are proactively alerted to potential risks, enabling timely action to mitigate these risks.

The "Recommendation" section on the dashboard lists these suppliers, allowing buyers to create findings or ignore based on the intelligent analysis provided. A new "Recommendation" section has been added to the SAP Ariba Supplier Risk dashboard. Pop-ups detailing the percentage change in risk score and action options (create finding/ignore) are now available for each recommended supplier.

SAP Ariba Supplier Risk dashboard new recommendations section

Self-Assessed Questionnaire Assessment Notification To All Relevant Suppliers

Buyers can now send Self-Assessed Questionnaires to both registered and non-registered suppliers in SAP Business Network, ensuring comprehensive participation and data collection. Suppliers receive an email invitation with a direct link to the questionnaire, facilitating quick and easy completion. The Engagement request page now includes a "Supplier type" filter (All, SBN, Non SBN) for differentiating suppliers based on their registration status in SAP Business Network.

SAP Business Network Certificate Information in the Supplier Profile Summary

This feature provides added visibility into supplier-related certificates maintained in SAP Business Network profiles. These certificates are shown in a new “Business Network Certificates” panel, providing additional data points for supplier assessments. Clicking the panel opens the supplier's SAP Business Network profile in a new tab, displaying certificate details.

Business Network Certificates panel in the supplier profile summary

Custom Risk Categories in the Supplier Profile Summary

The inclusion of custom risk categories in supplier profile summaries allows organizations to consider risk factors unique to their requirements. The supplier profile summary now include exposure information for the top four custom categories, providing a more tailored view of supplier risk exposure. Support is available for up to 25 custom risk categories, and the top four categories by weight (or alphabetically if weights are equal) are now displayed in the Risk Exposure area of the profile summary banner.

Ability to Save Internal Supplier Registration Forms as Draft

Users now have the option to save their progress on internal supplier registration questionnaires, allowing them to return and complete the form at their convenience without losing data. This feature simplifies the process, reducing the likelihood of incomplete submissions. A new "SAVE" option on internal forms enables users to save all answers as drafts.

These exciting features are aimed at enhancing supplier management, improving risk detection, and providing more visibility into supplier credentials. We encourage you to explore these new enhancements and integrate them into your workflows for maximum benefit. Stay tuned for more updates and insightful blogs. Don't forget to subscribe to our bloggers for the latest updates!

Resources:

    • Register for the Early Release Series for 2411 release
    • Resources:
      • Feature related materials
        • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
        • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
      • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
      • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network 
      • What’s New
    • Check out our content on youtube!
      • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
      • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M
  •

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2411 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We're thrilled to announce some fantastic new features and enhancements for Q4 2024 across various SAP Ariba modules. These updates are designed to streamline your procurement processes, enhance supplier management, and provide deeper insights into supplier relationships. Let's take a high-level look at the most impactful innovations in this release.

For those of you wanting to explore specific functionalities in detail, we have provided links to dedicated blogs that dive deeper into each area. Let's take a look!

SAP Spend Control Tower

We're excited to share the latest development with you SAP Spend Control Tower. This new tool is designed to merge all your payment and supplier data into a single source of truth, enriched with AI-enabled spend classification and supplier insights. With intuitive dashboards and advanced machine learning models, it’s easier than ever to streamline our processes, cut procurement costs, manage supplier risks, and keep an eye on our diversity and sustainability goals.

Having a unified data source might just be the key to overcoming some of our biggest challenges. SAP Spend Control Tower takes the powerful analytics and reporting capabilities of the old SAP Ariba Spend Analysis solution and pushes them even further. If you're curious to learn more about how it could help us transform our spend management, I'd recommend checking out the full details on our SAP Spend Control Tower product page.

Spend overview dashboard in SAP Spend Control Tower

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance and Supplier Risk

Intelligent Recommendations Based on Supplier Risk Score

SAP Ariba Supplier Risk now offers intelligent recommendations for suppliers experiencing significant changes in risk scores. These new insights help buyers proactively identify and mitigate potential risks.

New recommendations section in the SAP Ariba Supplier Risk dashboard

SAP Business Network Certificate Information in the Supplier Profile Summary

The supplier profile summary now features added visibility into certificates maintained in SAP Business Network profiles. The new “Business Network Certificates” panel provides additional data points for supplier assessments, offering a comprehensive view directly from their SAP Business Network profile.

Business network certificates in the supplier profile summary

See the SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance & Supplier Risk 2411 Release: Key Feature Highlights blog for more information on these and other supplier management features

SAP Ariba Source to Contract

Enhancements to Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP Ariba Category Management

We're excited to introduce enhancements to our Generative AI capabilities in SAP Ariba Category Management. You can now selectively generate articles for tools like Category Segmentation, Market Dynamics, and Cost Structure on demand, improving performance and reducing costs associated with producing AI-generated content.

Enhancements to Generative Artificial Intelligence in SAP Ariba Category Management

Ability to Create Category Strategy Details from the Develop Strategy and Plan App in SAP Ariba Category Management

Our new Category Strategy Details tool in the Develop Strategy and Plan app empowers category managers to create and manage multiple category-specific strategy details aligned with the overall business strategy.

Category strategy details in SAP Ariba Category Management

Ability to View, Analyze, and Manage Initiatives Across Categories in SAP Ariba Category Management

Manage your initiatives through the new Initiatives Overview app, providing comprehensive tools for tracking, optimizing, and visualizing data.

Initiatives overview in SAP Ariba Category Management

 

Create Optimization Scenarios for Multi-Round Bidding Events in Guided Sourcing

Generate optimization scenarios for multi-round bidding events, including editing constraints and updating scenarios with the latest data.

Enhancements to Grading and Scoring in Guided Sourcing

Improved grading features for guided sourcing, including offline grading for primary bids and rolled-up section level grading for alternate bids.

See the SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2411 Release Key Innovations Preview blog for more information on these and other source to contract features

SAP Ariba Procure to Order

Support for PunchOut Items in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App

You can now purchase items from external supplier catalogs through Level 1 PunchOut catalogs directly from the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app. This enhancement broadens your product range for mobile shopping while ensuring compliance and approvals are managed through the normal Ariba checkout process.

View and Edit Requisition Line Items in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App

The SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app now provides detailed line item information in the requisition details screen and allows users to edit line items directly from the checkout screen. This update enhances flexibility and transparency in the mobile requisitioning process.

See the SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2411 Release Key Innovations blog for more information on these and other features

For further reading and resources, check out the links below and dive deeper into each specific innovation.

Resources:
    • Register for the Early Release Series for 2411 release
    • Resources:
      • Feature related materials
        • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
        • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
      • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
      • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network 
      • What’s New
    • Check out our content on youtube!
      • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
      • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: How to Use Splunk for Data Analysis in SAP Ariba Applications: A Beginner's Guide. Part 1
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome to the comprehensive beginner's guide on using Splunk for Data Analysis in SAP Ariba Application. This guide is divided into several parts, each providing detailed instructions for understanding the main features and functionality of the Splunk tool. Without further ado, let's embark on the journey to enhance data analysis capabilities in SAP Ariba.

What is the Splunk?

Splunk is an essential data analysis tool that plays a crucial role in aggregating data for analysis and providing logs. Particularly for SAP Ariba, it allows the monitoring of every aspect of the application processes, specifically  to procurement and supply chain. By doing so, it can help in tracking and analyzing transaction data, monitoring system performance, identifying security threa, and gaining visibility into operational inefficiencies. Moreover, Splunk provides the capability to proactively identify and resolve issues, optimize processes, and make data-driven decisions to improve overall efficiency and effectiveness.

One of the key features of Splunk is that it is comprised of several different applications. These apps essentially consist of a collection of files containing data inputs, UI elements, and knowledge objects. This flexibility with different apps allows for various use cases and user roles to coexist within a single Splunk instance.

 Splunk typically retains logs for a period ranging from 90 to 390 days, depending on the data center and the specific index. This ensures that the data is accessible for analysis and reference within the desired timeframe.

The Splunk access URL is determined by the SAP Ariba component and data center.

List of all Splunk and Inspector Links for every component by datacenter

It is important to note that the access to Splunk is only possible with Safeguard account and only SAP employees have access to Splunk tool.

 

Key components of the Splunk platform

Indexer.

  • Processes machine data from any source
  • Parses data by an organized source type (index)
  • This raw log data is broken up into single events
  • Events are stored by the index so they can be searched
  • Run the actual search done by user

Search Head

  • Allows users to use Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL) to search indexed data
  • Distributes the search to the Indexers
  • Extracts field value pairs from events to the user
  • Can extract additional fields (i.e. regex) or transform data (i.e. stats commands) for results before it is returned to end user without changing the underlying data
  • Provide tools for reports/dashboards/visualizations

Forwarder

  • Installed on servers where data originates
  • Primary way data is supplied to indexers for indexing

    Splunk’s Architecture

    The Cloud Health 2.0 App

    The Cloud Health 2.0 App is one of the key tool within the Splunk platform, utilized by various SAP Ariba teams for analyzing application log data. This app provides support and development teams with the capability to examine log data from any SAP Ariba component

    One of the distinguishing features of the Cloud Health 2.0 App is its multitude of customized, pre-extracted and calculated fields, such as AribaThread, AribaPrealm, and Community. These fields are exclusively available within the Cloud Health 2.0 app knowledge object, enabling users to leverage them in search queries.

    In addition to field extractions, the knowledge objects within the Cloud Health 2.0 App encompass lookups, fields, and alerts, providing a comprehensive toolkit for effective analysis and monitoring of log data.

    The app's value is underscored by the fact that utilizing these specific fields in the default search app of Splunk – the search and reporting app – yields no results unless the corresponding knowledge object exists within it.

    Another benefit of the Cloud 2.0 app for users is that all dashboards and alert knowledge objects are shared across global data centers.

    Main page

    The opening of the cloud health signifies the importance of the application, which is prominently positioned at the top of the black Splunk menu bar. This feature is accessible from any Splunk application, providing users with ease of access. Within the aforementioned bar, users can find the application chooser, which allows for seamless switching between different apps. This centralized location serves as a convenient hub for app navigation.Furthermore, each application contains its own specific navigation features within the bar, ensuring a tailored user experience for each individual app.

    If someone were to wish to run a simple, ad hoc search, they could do so by clicking on the search button. This action would subsequently open the search screen in a new tab, displaying a page with the desired results. Users can then input their search criteria into the search bar, beginning with "index equals," followed by the specified index. In some cases, users may also be searching for a specific string of text.

    Upon running the search and obtaining results, a bar chart depicting the event timeline will be displayed. This chart effectively illustrates the distribution of events across time intervals. The specific timing of these intervals will adjust dynamically depending on the length of the chosen time frame for the search.

    Each individual result is considered an event, with different fields and values attributed to each one. For example, the "host" field is accompanied by a corresponding value. Additionally, there are various job functions located on the top right-hand side of the page.

Search example

The time zone displayed for specific events is based on user preferences and can be adjusted according to individual needs. To do so, simply click on your name, navigate to the preferences section, then select the global settings and change the time zone as desired. This allows users to ensure events and appointments are accurately reflected in their own local time.

Timezone

Search Assistant

The search assistant is a helpful tool that offers various selections and options for completing a search. Users have the ability to select a term from a list, continue typing, and choose between default, compact, or full view. To access or deactivate the search assistant, users must click on their name, navigate to preferences, select SPL editor, and then the search assistant.

Preferences

The compact mode provides command information as users type and displays the last five queries for quick selection. In full mode, the display provides more information and includes more text as well as examples. However, the choice of which view to use ultimately depends on the user's preference.

Search Assistant Compact Mode

Search Assistant Full mode

The next topic for consideration is the selection of time in the Search feature. When it comes to selecting the time, there are several different options available. One option is to select the time from a time picker. Another option is that real-time search is only allowed for Power/Admin users. Additionally, time can be defined in the search query using various options such as "earliest" or "latest." Time notation is also available, with "s" representing seconds, "m" representing minutes, "h" representing hours, "d" representing days, "w" representing weeks, "mon" representing months, and "y" representing years.

 Selecting Time in Search

 

In order to view a specific subset of events using the search feature, users can click on a particular bar. This action filters the search results to display only the events related to the selected bar. To clear the filter, simply clicking on any part of the white space in the bar will remove it.

Search Example

Controlling the jobs allows for various options such as pausing a job while it is still running and stopping a job if needed. Additionally, the default duration for active search jobs is 10 minutes, but they can be shared with others via a URL that remains active for seven days. The search results can be downloaded in different formats such as CSV, JSON, XML, or raw text. There are three different search modes available: fast, which only returns default field data or any required fields for the search; verbose, which returns a large amount of data and may impact search performance if used for large queries; and smart, which recommends field discovery for searches and adds new fields if Splunk determines there is enough data to add them. The smart mode also does not provide event or field data for stats searches. Overall, these options provide flexibility and control in managing search jobs and their results.

 Controlling Jobs

In order to access previous job and search history, users can click on the activity section and view jobs by the owner application or status. The search history can be found at the bottom of the search page, where users can expand and view their previous searches. This feature is particularly useful for users who may have forgotten to save a query they ran a few days ago. The search history page is designed to retain data for up to 30 days, allowing users to access any searches conducted within that timeframe.

History

Conclusion

Having finished the basic overview of the Splunk tool and its main features for SAP Ariba data analysis.  On next parts will continue to dive to this tool and will talk about Dashboards and Alerts & Schedulated Reports. . The aim of this guide is to be helpful and aid in making comprehensive analysis of any Ariba issues on a daily basis. Should you have any questions or need further assistance, do not hesitate to reach out.

SAP Ariba Sourcing  SAP Ariba Invoice Management  SAP Ariba Spend Analysis SAP Ariba Supplier Risk #splunk

entry.title: Release 2411 - What´s coming ?
entry.content:
It´s always important to understand the content of Releases. You can have an excellent idea about what´s coming and links to access the Roadmap for Best Practices.

There will be enhancements in 2411 for Spend Control Tower, Supplier Management (SLP and Risk), SAP Business Network, Category Management  and Guided Sourcing. Enjoy

Go to this link . https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-ariba-2411-release-key-innovations-preview/ba-p/13916252

Remember that you can use the Language translation in your browser (if allowed) to see the text more easily (click the right button over the text).

entry.title: 2502 Early Release Series for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network releases
entry.content:
Sign up now for the 2502 Early Release Series

Follow this blog to receive notification of new series as soon as registration opens.

SAP strives to equip our customers and partners with valuable release readiness resources for each SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network quarterly release.

Early Release Series

The SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Early Release series of webcasts cover features planned for the upcoming quarterly release.  This platform, available to customers and partners, has a clear mission: deliver an interactive forum to engage with SAP product experts providing simple, direct answers to your most pressing questions.  There is a different session for each product area with content tailored for the specific audience:

  • Business Network
  • Supplier Management & Risk and Procure to Pay
  • Source to Contract

If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts, we encourage you to participate in these informative sessions.  Register now!

Quarterly Release Communications

Notice of upcoming quarterly releases will be communicated to Buyer customers three weeks prior to the release date.  Suppliers receive notice two weeks prior.  Releases are communicated via email notification (look for subject line starting with: Advance Release Notice).

Anyone from your organization that has chosen to subscribe to product notifications will receive these announcements. 

The release calendar for SAP Business Network and release calendar SAP Ariba Applications identify release timing several quarters in advance.

 Where to find out more details?

On demand replays of webcasts from completed quarterly releases can be consumed from Release Readiness Resources: Webcast Archive Page

entry.title: What's New in 2502 - Release Navigator for Spend Management and Business Network
entry.content:
The Readiness@Scale team is excited to announce that the first release of 2025 is coming up quickly! In 2024, the Release Navigators were accessed over 90,000 times to prepare for SAP releases. Make sure to visit the 2502 edition of the Release Navigator for Spend Management and Business Network on January 27th for updated release content to ensure a success release. This update will showcase the latest content to keep you informed and prepared for the newest innovations. Access all essential resources in one convenient location for all of Spend Management and Business Network including What’s New Viewer, Release Blogs, Webcasts, Release Calendars, Blogs, Tutorial Videos, and more. Click the link below to easily and efficiently prepare for the 2502 Release!

  • SC-22437 Decommissioning of Next Gen UI for CLID in SAP Ariba Contracts: This feature removes the ability to use the Next Gen UI for CLID in SAP Ariba Contracts. Customers must use the classic user interface, available by default, to manage Contract Line Items Documents (CLID) in SAP Ariba Contracts.
  • Depreciation for weak TLS 1.2 cipher suite connections and enable support for TLS 1.3: Starting Friday, January 24, 2025, there will be a change to the SAP Ariba applications and SAP Business Network supported cipher suite connections.  On this date, new TLS cipher suite connection security standards will be implemented; support for weak TLS 1.2 connections will end and the support of TLS 1.3 connections will begin.

Making Your Impact

At SAP, we greatly appreciate your input and suggestions. You are invited to submit feedback and suggest topics to be covered through our Topic Request & Feedback Survey. This survey provides you with an opportunity to provide insights on your experience with the release navigator for Spend Management and Business Network, as well as suggestions on how we can further enhance your release experience. Additionally, we encourage you to share your feedback, thoughts, or questions in the comment section below. 

Staying Connected

Follow the Spend Management and Business Network and ReadinessS@Scale community tags, and Ryan Russman to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: SAP Finance and Spend Management - Cloud Onboarding Services
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the launch of a new Continuous Quality Check service (CQC) specifically for Customer Onboarding! Now all customers can request a 1:1 Onboarding session covering important topics such as initial system access and s-user setup, SAP for Me, SAP Support, learning and enablement options, implementation planning, and product innovation.

This offering will be extremely valuable both to new customers looking to kickstart their implementations as well as existing customers who need a refresher. The CQC is currently available for SAP Ariba, SAP Fieldglass and SAP Business Network. For more information, access the one pager on the CQC page.
